import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate.PresheafInternalHom

/-!
# Dual-inverse parallel lane (A.1.c.SubT ¬ßDual, iter-251)

This file holds the **dual-inverse chain** that feeds `exists_tensorObj_inverse` in
`TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:

1. `dual_restrict_iso` ‚Äî restriction along an open immersion commutes with the sheaf-level
   dual (blueprint `lem:dual_restrict_iso`; the C-bridge).  **CLOSED** (iter-317, axiom-clean):
   Steps 1‚Äì3 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`/`sheafificationCompPullback`/strip) + H1
   (`pushforwardPushforwardAdj`‚àò`leftAdjointUniq`) + the Step-4 presheaf residual
   `(pushforward Œ≤).obj (dual M.val) ‚âÖ dual ((pushforward Œ≤).obj M.val)`
   are all in place.  Step-4 is assembled sectionwise from `sliceDualTransport` (see piece 1b
   below) via `isoMk`; its `Opens Y` V-naturality square closes by `rfl` after splitting the
   composites (the transport `.app`/`restrictionMap`/pushforward-`dual.map` reindexings are all
   defeq, so both legs land on `œÜ.app` at the same slice object).

   1b. `sliceDualTransport` ‚Äî the per-`V` `ùí™_Y(V)`-linear iso of the Step-4 residual.  **PARTIAL**
   (iter-262): the obligation is a `ùí™_Y(V)`-linear equivalence between the two morphism (`Hom`)
   types `(restr fV' M.val ‚ü∂ restr fV' ùüô_X)` (restricted along `Œ≤.app V`) and
   `(restr V ((pushforward Œ≤).obj M.val) ‚ü∂ restr V ùüô_Y)`, where `fV' = f.opensFunctor.obj V`.
   ROUTE-1 (consume the shared root `Scheme.Modules.overEquivalence`/`restrictOverIso`/`unitOverIso`)
   is **STRUCTURALLY DEAD** (iter-260): those are `restrict‚Ü¶over` / `unit‚Ü¶unit` SHEAF isos ‚Äî they say
   nothing about `dual`; producing the dual-commutation they lack needs the avoided
   `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules)`.  The genuine close is the direct sectionwise build
   (ROUTE-2, sanctioned iter-261): leg-A reindexes `œÜ` across `f.opensFunctor` (categorical
   `restrictScalars ‚Ä¶ |>.map`), leg-B swaps the codomain unit ring via `dualUnitRingSwap`
   (= `inv (Œµ (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom))`).  **Leg-B is CLOSED (iter-262)** as the named
   `dualUnitRingSwap` + `isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso` (recipe `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); the
   `codomainMap` hole is filled by defeq.  `map_add'` is CLOSED (iter-263) and `map_smul'` is CLOSED
   (iter-264, axiom-clean: Œ≤-naturality ring identity `s = (Œ≤.app W').hom c` via
   `Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_naturality` + `ùí™_Y(W')`-linearity of `dualUnitRingSwap.hom` via `map_smul`).
   `invFun` is CLOSED (iter-265/271, extracted as `sliceDualTransportInv`), and `left_inv`/`right_inv`
   are CLOSED (iter-313, axiom-clean).  The forward `naturality` field is CLOSED (iter-316,
   axiom-clean) via the extracted standalone lemma `sliceDualTransport_toFun_naturality` (own
   heartbeat budget; leg-A `œà`/`œÜ`-`naturality_apply` ‚àò leg-B `appIso_hom_naturality_apply`, the
   verified `.hom'`-wall `erw` recipe).  The reverse `sliceDualTransportInv.naturality` is CLOSED
   (iter-317, axiom-clean): the reverse Œµ-telescope reduces (collapse-clears + `ŒµInv` + `appIso`-swap
   reductions) to a 3-identity residual ‚Äî the cross-fiber `M.val.map` alignment `harg`
   (parallel thin-poset paths via `congr_map_apply`+`Subsingleton.elim`), `PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply œà`,
   and the leg-B `appIso_inv_naturality` relabel chase `keyB`.  **All `sliceDualTransport`/`Inv` fields and
   `dual_restrict_iso` are now sorry-free.**
2. `dual_isLocallyTrivial` ‚Äî the dual of a locally-trivial module is locally trivial
   (blueprint `lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial`).  **CLOSED** (iter-317; `dual_restrict_iso` is now
   sorry-free): the three-step chart-chase
   `dual_restrict_iso ‚â™‚â´ (dualIsoOfIso eL).symm ‚â™‚â´ dual_unit_iso` is assembled and compiles, no
   longer inheriting any residual.  The third leg `dual_unit_iso`
   and its presheaf core `presheafDualUnitIso` (= the ¬ß0 `dualUnitIsoGen`, the eval-at-`1`
   `dual ùüô_ ‚âÖ ùüô_`) are built axiom-clean.
3. `homOfLocalCompat` ‚Äî a compatible family of local `ùí™_X`-module morphisms over an open
   cover glues to a unique global morphism (blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat`;
   the A-bridge).  **CLOSED** (iter-256), axiom-clean; the multi-piece sheaf-of-homs gluing
   engine.  The final sub-step (c) sectionwise `ùí™_X`-linearity is closed by the native‚Üî
   `restrictScalars ùüô` smul bridge `hbridge` (from `Scheme.Opens.Œπ_appIso` +
   `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def'`), feeding the native f-leg linearity `hfl_native`.

The prover lane for this file works **in parallel** with the D1‚Ä≤/D3‚Ä≤/D4‚Ä≤ lane in
`TensorObjSubstrate.lean`.

Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory

/-! ## ¬ß0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit

Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual ùüô_ ‚âÖ ùüô_`
(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `‚Ñãom(ùüô_, ùüô_) ‚âÖ ùüô_`), built over a general single-universe
base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R‚ÇÄ := Y.presheaf`. -/

namespace PresheafOfModules

open InternalHom Opposite

variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R‚ÇÄ : D·µí·µñ ‚•§ CommRingCat.{u}}

/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
(restricted) unit `restr X ùüô_ ‚ü∂ restr X ùüô_` are identified `R‚ÇÄ(X)`-linearly with `R‚ÇÄ(X)` itself,
via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
at `1` (proved from `œÜ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : D·µí·µñ) :
    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
      (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))
    (restr X.unop (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))) ‚ü∂
        restr X.unop (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))))
      ‚âÉ‚Çó[(R‚ÇÄ.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R‚ÇÄ.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
    (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))
    (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))
  exact
    { toFun := fun œÜ =>
        evalLin (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))) X œÜ
          (1 : ((R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
      map_add' := fun œÜ œÜ' => rfl
      map_smul' := fun c œÜ => by
        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c œÜ)
          (1 : ((R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
      invFun := fun r =>
        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
          (restr X.unop (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
      left_inv := fun œÜ => by
        ext Y
        dsimp only
        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply œÜ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
          (1 : ((R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
        rfl
      right_inv := fun r => by
        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
            (restr X.unop
              (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
            (op (Over.mk (ùüô X.unop)))).hom
            (1 : ((R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
        exact mul_one r }

/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual ùüô_ ‚âÖ ùüô_`,
assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = ùüô_`). -/
noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
    PresheafOfModules.dual (R‚ÇÄ := R‚ÇÄ)
        (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))
      ‚âÖ ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
    (fun {X Y} f => by
      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun œÜ => ?_)
      change evalLin (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
              (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f œÜ)
            (1 : ((R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
          = ((ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
              (evalLin (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))) X œÜ
                (1 : ((R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
        (œÜ : restr X.unop (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))) ‚ü∂
          restr X.unop (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))))
        (Over.homMk f.unop (Category.comp_id _) : Over.mk f.unop ‚ü∂ Over.mk (ùüô X.unop)).op
        (1 : ((R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
      have hrm : (restr X.unop
            (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
          (Over.homMk f.unop (Category.comp_id _) : Over.mk f.unop ‚ü∂ Over.mk (ùüô X.unop)).op
          = (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
      rw [hrm] at key
      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (ùüô Y.unop ‚â´ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)·µí·µñ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
      have homAppHEq : ‚àÄ {A B : (Over X.unop)·µí·µñ} (_ : A = B), HEq (œÜ.app A) (œÜ.app B) := by
        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
      have hdt : evalLin (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
            (ùüô_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R‚ÇÄ ‚ãô forget‚ÇÇ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f œÜ)
          = (œÜ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)

end PresheafOfModules

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

namespace Modules

/-! ## ¬ßA. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/

open Opposite in
/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `Œµ` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion
structure ring iso `(f.appIso W').inv` is an isomorphism.**  Its underlying map is the (bijective)
ring map `(f.appIso W').inv.hom`, so `Œµ` is an iso by `restrictScalars_isIso_Œµ_of_bijective`
(`PresheafInternalHom.lean`) fed the bijectivity from `ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`.  This
is the single load-bearing fact powering `dualUnitRingSwap` (the codomain unit ring swap of leg-B),
phrased at the `CommRingCat` carrier so `CommRing` is native (per `analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
lemma isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y) :
    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)) :=
  restrictScalars_isIso_Œµ_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom
    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv)

/-- **Element action of `inv Œµ` for a `restrictScalars` along a bijective ring hom.**  The
lax-monoidal unit `Œµ (restrictScalars g)` has underlying map `g` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_Œ∑`);
since `g` is bijective `Œµ` is invertible (`restrictScalars_isIso_Œµ_of_bijective`) and the underlying
map of `inv Œµ` is `g‚Åª¬π` (`(RingEquiv.ofBijective g hg).symm`).  This is the reusable element-level
ingredient that powers the Œµ-swap cancellations in the `sliceDualTransport` round-trips and
naturality (`dualUnitRingSwap`/`dualUnitRingSwapHom`/`unitRelabelSwap` are all `inv Œµ`s). -/
lemma ŒµInv_apply {R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (g : R ‚Üí+* S)
    (hg : Function.Bijective g) (s : S) :
    haveI := restrictScalars_isIso_Œµ_of_bijective g hg
    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.hom
        (CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g)))) s
      = (RingEquiv.ofBijective g hg).symm s := by
  haveI := restrictScalars_isIso_Œµ_of_bijective g hg
  have key : (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.hom
        (CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g))))
        ((CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.hom (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g)))
          ((RingEquiv.ofBijective g hg).symm s)) = (RingEquiv.ofBijective g hg).symm s := by
    rw [‚Üê CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, IsIso.hom_inv_id]; rfl
  rw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_Œ∑] at key
  rw [show g ((RingEquiv.ofBijective g hg).symm s) = s from
    (RingEquiv.ofBijective g hg).apply_symm_apply s] at key
  exact key

open Opposite in
/-- **Two Œµ-swap cancellation on the unit carrier.**  The reverse transport `sliceDualTransportInv`
applies `inv Œµ (.hom-direction)` after the forward transport's `inv Œµ (.inv-direction)`; on the
shared section ring `ùí™_X(f''·µÅP)` the two `inv Œµ` (= `(RingEquiv.ofBijective (appIso).hom).symm` and
`(RingEquiv.ofBijective (appIso).inv).symm`) cancel, because `(appIso f P).hom` and `(appIso f P).inv`
are mutually-inverse ring maps (`Iso.hom_inv_id`).  Stated at the plain `‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj _)` carrier
(not the `restr`/`ùüô_` spelling) so the `RingEquiv`/`Mul` instance synthesis is native; the caller
bridges the unit-object spelling with `erw`. -/
lemma appIso_swap_cancel {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (P : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y)
    (hh : Function.Bijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom))
    (hi : Function.Bijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).inv))
    (u : ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj P)))) :
    (RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom) hh).symm
        ((RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).inv) hi).symm u) = u := by
  have h1 : (RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).inv) hi).symm u
      = (RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom) hh) u := by
    rw [RingEquiv.symm_apply_eq, RingEquiv.ofBijective_apply, RingEquiv.ofBijective_apply]
    have hki := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom_inv_id
    simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_id] at hki
    exact (RingHom.congr_fun hki u).symm
  rw [h1, RingEquiv.symm_apply_apply]

open Opposite in
/-- **`inv Œµ`-relabel as the reverse section restriction map.**  For an `eqToHom`-induced section
relabel `X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)` (`e : a = b` of section opens), the inverse `RingEquiv`
(produced by `ŒµInv_apply` at the unit-relabel swap `unitRelabelSwap`) is just the reverse relabel
`X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e.symm)`.  Lets the `unitRelabelSwap` `inv Œµ` in the `sliceDualTransport`
round-trips collapse to a plain presheaf restriction, exposing `œÜ.naturality`. -/
lemma presheafMap_ofBijective_symm {X : Scheme.{u}}
    {a b : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ} (e : a = b)
    (hb : Function.Bijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e))))
    (s : ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj b)) :
    (RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e))) hb).symm s
      = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e.symm))) s := by
  rw [RingEquiv.symm_apply_eq, RingEquiv.ofBijective_apply, ‚Üê CommRingCat.comp_apply,
    ‚Üê Functor.map_comp, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, X.presheaf.map_id, ConcreteCategory.id_apply]

open Opposite in
/-- **Three-way `appIso` relabel cancellation (the `right_inv` analogue of `appIso_swap_cancel`).**
In `right_inv` the `Œµrel` section-relabel sits BETWEEN the two `appIso` swaps (unlike `left_inv`,
where they are adjacent and cancel via `appIso_swap_cancel`).  The composite
`(appIso W').hom ‚àò (X-side relabel `X.presheaf.map (opensFunctor.op.map i)`) ‚àò (appIso P).inv`
collapses to the Y-side section relabel `Y.presheaf.map i`, via the naturality of `appIso.inv`
against restriction (`Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_naturality`) followed by `Iso.inv_hom_id` of `appIso`. -/
lemma appIso_relabel_cancel {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    {P W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y} (i : (Opposite.op P : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y)·µí·µñ) ‚ü∂ op W')
    (u : ‚Üë(Y.presheaf.obj (op P))) :
    (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom)
        ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (X.presheaf.map ((Hom.opensFunctor f).op.map i)))
          ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).inv) u))
      = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Y.presheaf.map i)) u := by
  -- Naturality of `appIso.inv` against the relabel `i`, packaged as a morphism identity.
  have hnat := Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_naturality f i
  have key : (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).inv ‚â´ X.presheaf.map ((Hom.opensFunctor f).op.map i) ‚â´
        (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom = Y.presheaf.map i := by
    rw [‚Üê Category.assoc, ‚Üê hnat]
    erw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
  have hu := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom key u
  erw [CommRingCat.comp_apply, CommRingCat.comp_apply] at hu
  exact hu

open Opposite in
/-- **`(ofBijective (appIso W').inv).symm = (appIso W').hom` at the element level.**  The inverse of
the `RingEquiv` built from `(appIso W').inv` is the underlying map of `(appIso W').hom`, by
`hom_inv_id` of `appIso`.  A spelling-bridge for the `right_inv`/`left_inv` round-trips. -/
lemma ofBijective_appIso_inv_symm {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y)
    (hi : Function.Bijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv))
    (x : ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W')))) :
    (RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv) hi).symm x
      = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom) x := by
  rw [RingEquiv.symm_apply_eq, RingEquiv.ofBijective_apply]
  have h := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom_inv_id
  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_id] at h
  exact (RingHom.congr_fun h x).symm

open Opposite in
/-- **`(ofBijective (appIso P).hom).symm = (appIso P).inv` at the element level.**  Mirror of
`ofBijective_appIso_inv_symm` for the `.hom`-direction swap, by `inv_hom_id` of `appIso`. -/
lemma ofBijective_appIso_hom_symm {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (P : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y)
    (hh : Function.Bijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom))
    (x : ‚Üë(Y.presheaf.obj (op P))) :
    (RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom) hh).symm x
      = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).inv) x := by
  rw [RingEquiv.symm_apply_eq, RingEquiv.ofBijective_apply]
  have h := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).inv_hom_id
  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_id] at h
  exact (RingHom.congr_fun h x).symm

open Opposite in
/-- **Three-way relabel cancellation, in the `RingEquiv.ofBijective ¬∑.symm` spelling of the goal.**
Exactly the telescope appearing in the `right_inv` round-trip after `erw [ŒµInv_apply √ó3]`:
`(ofBij (appIso W').inv).symm ‚àò (ofBij (X-relabel)).symm ‚àò (ofBij (appIso P).hom).symm` applied to a
section `u : ùí™_Y(P)` collapses to the `Y`-side section relabel `Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom (op hPW))`
(`hPW : P = W'`).  Proved by `subst hPW`, after which both relabels are identities and the two
`appIso` swaps cancel via `inv_hom_id`. -/
lemma appIso_relabel_cancel_apply {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    {P W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y} (hPW : P = W')
    (eX : (op ((Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W') : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ)
      = op ((Hom.opensFunctor f).obj P))
    (hi : Function.Bijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv))
    (hr : Function.Bijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom eX))))
    (hh : Function.Bijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom))
    (u : ‚Üë(Y.presheaf.obj (op P))) :
    (RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv) hi).symm
        ((RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom eX))) hr).symm
          ((RingEquiv.ofBijective (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom) hh).symm u))
      = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op hPW)))) u := by
  subst hPW
  rw [ofBijective_appIso_hom_symm, presheafMap_ofBijective_symm, ofBijective_appIso_inv_symm]
  -- both `eX`-relabels are now identities (`eqToHom` between equal opens)
  rw [eqToHom_refl, X.presheaf.map_id, eqToHom_refl, Y.presheaf.map_id]
  simp only [CommRingCat.id_apply]
  -- residual: `(appIso P).hom ((appIso P).inv u) = u`
  have h := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).inv_hom_id
  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_id] at h
  exact RingHom.congr_fun h u

/-- **The double-`restrictScalars` collapse legs act as the identity on elements.**  Both
`ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App` and `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` are `AddEquiv.refl` on the
underlying carrier, so the `sliceDualTransportInv` `?collapse` morphism
`(restrictScalarsId'App (g‚àòf) ‚ãØ M).inv ‚â´ (restrictScalarsComp'App f g (g‚àòf) rfl M).hom` is the
identity at the element level (`rfl`).  Used to clear the collapse legs in `sliceDualTransport.left_inv`
(where, unlike `right_inv`, the absence of a pushforward means `PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply`
cannot absorb them by defeq).  Proof-irrelevance makes the concrete `Id'App` hypothesis match. -/
lemma restrictScalars_collapse_apply {R‚ÇÅ R‚ÇÇ : Type u} [Ring R‚ÇÅ] [Ring R‚ÇÇ]
    (f : R‚ÇÅ ‚Üí+* R‚ÇÇ) (g : R‚ÇÇ ‚Üí+* R‚ÇÅ) (hgf : g.comp f = RingHom.id R‚ÇÅ)
    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R‚ÇÅ) (x : ‚ÜëM) :
    (((ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App (g.comp f) hgf M).inv ‚â´
        (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App f g (g.comp f) rfl M).hom)).hom' x = x := rfl

open Opposite in
/-- **Leg-B: the codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (ùüô_X(fW')) ‚ü∂
ùüô_Y(W')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `Œµ (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)`,
an isomorphism by `isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso`.  The endpoints are written at the canonical
`CommRingCat` section carriers `‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj _)` / `‚Üë(Y.presheaf.obj _)` (the `forget‚ÇÇ`-composite
carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis, `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); they reconcile by
`rfl`/defeq with the `restr`/`ùüô_`-section spellings of `sliceDualTransport`'s `codomainMap` hole. -/
noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y) :
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
        (ùüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) ‚ü∂
      ùüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Üë(Y.presheaf.obj (op W'))) :=
  haveI := isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))

open Opposite in
/-- **Leg-B (inverse direction): the unit codomain ring-iso swap for `invFun`** `ùüô_Y(W') ‚ü∂
restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (ùüô_X(fW'))`.  This is the lax-monoidal unit
`Œµ (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)` ITSELF (not its inverse), the reverse of
`dualUnitRingSwap`.  By `isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso` it is an isomorphism and is the inverse of
`dualUnitRingSwap f W'` (they cancel by `IsIso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id`). -/
noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwapInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y) :
    (ùüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Üë(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))) ‚ü∂
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
        (ùüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) :=
  Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)

open Opposite in
/-- `dualUnitRingSwapInv` is a section of `dualUnitRingSwap` (`Œµ ‚â´ inv Œµ = ùüô`). -/
@[simp] lemma dualUnitRingSwapInv_comp_dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y) :
    dualUnitRingSwapInv f W' ‚â´ dualUnitRingSwap f W' = ùüô _ := by
  haveI := isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
  simp [dualUnitRingSwapInv, dualUnitRingSwap]

open Opposite in
/-- `dualUnitRingSwap` is a section of `dualUnitRingSwapInv` (`inv Œµ ‚â´ Œµ = ùüô`). -/
@[simp] lemma dualUnitRingSwap_comp_dualUnitRingSwapInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y) :
    dualUnitRingSwap f W' ‚â´ dualUnitRingSwapInv f W' = ùüô _ := by
  haveI := isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
  simp [dualUnitRingSwapInv, dualUnitRingSwap]

open Opposite in
/-- **`invFun` codomain Œµ is an iso (`.hom` direction).**  The lax-monoidal unit `Œµ` of
`restrictScalars` along `(f.appIso W').hom` (the `.hom`, not `.inv`, of the structure ring iso) is
an isomorphism, since `(f.appIso W').hom` is a bijective ring map.  This powers the `invFun`
codomain swap (which reindexes the `Over V` section back across `f.opensFunctor` using the
`.hom` direction, the mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap`'s `.inv`). -/
lemma isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso_hom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y) :
    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom)) :=
  restrictScalars_isIso_Œµ_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom
    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom)

open Opposite in
/-- **`invFun` codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').hom (ùüô_Y(W')) ‚ü∂
ùüô_X(fW')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `Œµ (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').hom)`,
an isomorphism by `isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso_hom`.  This is the codomain swap of the reverse
transport `invFun` (mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap`, using the `.hom` direction). -/
noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwapHom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y) :
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom).obj
        (ùüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Üë(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))) ‚ü∂
      ùüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W')))) :=
  haveI := isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_appIso_hom f W'
  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').hom.hom))

open Opposite in
/-- **Œµ is an iso for the section-ring relabel** `X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)` (an `eqToHom`-induced,
hence bijective, ring map between section rings `ùí™_X(b) ‚Üí ùí™_X(a)` for `a = b`).  Phrased at the
`X.presheaf` (`CommRingCat`) carrier so `CommRing` is native (`analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
lemma isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_presheafMap {X : Scheme.{u}}
    {a b : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ} (e : a = b) :
    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom)) :=
  restrictScalars_isIso_Œµ_of_bijective (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom
    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)))

open Opposite in
/-- **Unit-section relabel swap** `restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)) (ùüô_X(b)) ‚ü∂ ùüô_X(a)`
for `a = b` (section opens of `X`).  It is `inv Œµ` of the relabel ring map, an isomorphism by
`isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_presheafMap`.  This is the `?unit` codomain transport of
`sliceDualTransportInv`'s reverse component (mirror of `dualUnitRingSwap` for the `he`-relabel). -/
noncomputable def unitRelabelSwap {X : Scheme.{u}}
    {a b : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•X)·µí·µñ} (e : a = b) :
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom).obj
        (ùüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj b))) ‚ü∂
      ùüô_ (ModuleCat ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj a)) :=
  haveI := isIso_Œµ_restrictScalars_presheafMap e
  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.Œµ
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom e)).hom))

set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
-- The reverse `app` 4-leg telescope (eqToHom/collapse/core/unitRelabelSwap) and its naturality
-- Œµ-paste overrun the default 200000-heartbeat budget during `whnf` on `restrictScalars`.
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
open PresheafOfModules InternalHom Opposite in
/-- **Reverse slice transport (the `invFun` of `sliceDualTransport`), extracted top-level.**

Given a dual section `œà : restr V ((pushforward Œ≤).obj M.val) ‚ü∂ restr V ùüô_Y` over `Over V`,
this produces the X-slice dual section `restr fV M.val ‚ü∂ restr fV ùüô_X` over `Over fV`
(`fV = f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`), the mirror of `sliceDualTransport`'s forward `toFun`.

For `W'' : (Over fV)·µí·µñ`, set `P := f‚Åª¬π·µÅ W''.left` (so `f.opensFunctor.obj P = W''.left` only
propositionally, via `image_preimage_of_le` since `fV ‚äÜ range f`).  The component at `W''` is the
X-slice mirror of the forward component, conjugated by the `eqToHom`s from `image_preimage_of_le`
(mirror of `homLocalSection`):
`eqToHom ‚Ä¶ ‚â´ (restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom).map (œà.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hPV)))) ‚â´
  dualUnitRingSwapHom f P`,
the codomain swap being `dualUnitRingSwapHom = inv (Œµ (restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom))`
(the `.hom`-direction `inv Œµ`). -/
noncomputable def sliceDualTransportInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ‚ü∂ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y)·µí·µñ)
    (Œ≤ : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ‚ü∂ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ‚ãô X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
    -- Œ≤-compatibility (iter-303): `Œ≤` is the open-immersion structure ring iso `(f.appIso).inv`,
    -- so post-composing it with `(f.appIso P).hom` is the identity on `ùí™_X(f''·µÅP)`.  This is the
    -- load-bearing ring identity that collapses the double `restrictScalars` in the reverse
    -- component (`?collapse`); it is FALSE for an arbitrary `Œ≤`, hence supplied as a hypothesis and
    -- discharged at the unique caller (`sliceDualTransport.invFun`) via `Iso.hom_inv_id`.
    (hŒ≤ : ‚àÄ (P : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y),
        ((Œ≤.app (op P)).hom).comp ((Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom.hom) = RingHom.id _)
    (œà : (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward Œ≤).obj M.val).dual.obj V : Type u)) :
    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward Œ≤).obj M.val.dual).obj V : Type u) := by
  refine { app := fun W'' => ?_, naturality := ?_ }
  ¬∑ -- app component at `W''` (over `fV`).  `W' := (unop W'').left ‚â§ fV`; `P := f‚Åª¬π·µÅ W'`.
    -- The down-set facts are established (axiom-clean); the morphism itself is the documented
    -- residual below.
    set W' := (unop W'').left with hW'
    have hW'fV : W' ‚â§ f ''·µÅ (unop V) := (unop W'').hom.le
    have hPV : f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W' ‚â§ unop V :=
      le_trans ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).monotone hW'fV)
        (le_of_eq (f.preimage_image_eq (unop V)))
    have he : f ''·µÅ (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W') = W' := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (hW'fV.trans (f.image_le_opensRange (unop V)))
    -- **app component ‚Äî CLOSED axiom-clean (iter-303).**  The X-slice mirror of the forward
    -- `toFun`, conjugated across the propositional preimage round-trip `he : f''·µÅ(f‚Åª¬π·µÅ W') = W'`.
    -- It is the four-leg composite (all legs concrete):
    --   (1) `M.val.map (eqToHom (op he.symm))` : source relabel `M.val(W') ‚ü∂ restr_œÅ M.val(fP)`
    --       (SEMILINEAR ‚Äî codomain restricted along `œÅ = X.ringCatSheaf.map (eqToHom (op he.symm))`,
    --       crossing the `ùí™_X(W') ‚Üî ùí™_X(fP)` fiber);
    --   (2) `restrictScalars œÅ |>.map (?collapse ‚â´ core)` transports the in-fiber-`fP` core:
    --       `?collapse` (the double-restrict collapse `M.val(fP) ‚âÖ restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom
    --       (restrictScalars (Œ≤.app P) (M.val fP))` via `restrictScalarsId'App` + `restrictScalarsComp'App`
    --       fed the ring identity `hŒ≤ (f‚Åª¬π·µÅ W')`), and `core` (legs (3) œà-reindex `restrictScalars
    --       (f.appIso P).hom |>.map (œà.app ‚Ä¶)` + (4) codomain unit swap `dualUnitRingSwapHom f P`);
    --   (3) `unitRelabelSwap (op he.symm)` : the codomain unit transport `restrictScalars œÅ ùüô_X(fP)
    --       ‚ü∂ ùüô_X(W')` (`inv Œµ` of the relabel, the new top-level helper).
    -- The cross-fiber transport (a single `‚â´`-chain cannot express it ‚Äî the relabel is semilinear)
    -- is realised by applying the functor `restrictScalars œÅ` to the in-fiber-`fP` core.
    -- **core (legs 3+4): VERIFIED well-formed in fiber `ùí™_X(fP)` (iter-303).**  The œà-reindex
    -- `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom ‚àò œà.app` post-composed with the codomain unit swap
    -- `dualUnitRingSwapHom f P` assembles into
    --   `core : restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom ((pushforward Œ≤ M.val)(P)) ‚ü∂ ùüô_X(fP)`,
    -- a morphism of `ModuleCat ùí™_X(fP)`.  (NB: the leg-3 target `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom
    -- ((restr V ùüô_Y)-section)` DID defeq-unify with leg-4's `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom
    -- (ùüô_ (ModuleCat ùí™_Y(P)))` ‚Äî the unit-spelling reconciles here, exactly as in the closed
    -- forward `toFun`.)
    have core := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W')).hom.hom).map
        (œà.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hPV)))) ‚â´ dualUnitRingSwapHom f (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W')
    -- **Cross-fiber transport ‚Äî CLOSED (iter-303).**  The goal lives in `ModuleCat ùí™_X(W')` but
    -- `core` lives in `ModuleCat ùí™_X(fP)` (`fP = f''·µÅf‚Åª¬π·µÅW'`, propositionally `= W'` via `he`, but
    -- the section RINGS `ùí™_X(W')` / `ùí™_X(fP)` are only propositionally equal).  The source relabel
    -- `M.val(W') ‚ü∂ M.val(fP)` is `M.val.map (eqToHom (op he.symm))` ‚Äî SEMILINEAR, landing in
    -- `restrictScalars (X.ringCatSheaf.map (eqToHom ‚Ä¶))`; combined with the source double-restrict
    -- collapse `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom ‚àò restrictScalars (Œ≤.app P) ‚âÖ restrictScalars ùüô
    -- ‚âÖ id` (ring identity `hŒ≤ (f‚Åª¬π·µÅ W')`: `(Œ≤.app P).hom ‚àò (f.appIso P).hom.hom = ùüô_{ùí™_X(fP)}`,
    -- collapsed by `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` + `restrictScalarsId'App`).  A single
    -- `‚â´`-chain in one `ModuleCat` cannot express this ‚Äî the relabel crosses ring fibers ‚Äî so `core`
    -- is conjugated across the `ùí™_X(fP) ‚Üî ùí™_X(W')` fiber by applying the functor
    -- `restrictScalars (X.ringCatSheaf.map (eqToHom (op he.symm)))` to `?collapse ‚â´ core` (per memory
    -- `ts271-slicedualtransportinv`).  This cross-fiber transport is the next fine-grained target.
    refine M.val.map (eqToHom (congrArg op he.symm)) ‚â´
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars ((X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (eqToHom (congrArg op he.symm))).hom)).map
        (?collapse ‚â´ core) ‚â´ ?unit
    case collapse =>
      -- Collapse the double `restrictScalars` on `M.val(fP)` to the identity, using the ring
      -- identity `hŒ≤ (f‚Åª¬π·µÅ W')` (`(Œ≤.app P).hom ‚àò (f.appIso P).hom = ùüô`).
      exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App _ (hŒ≤ (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W'))
            (M.val.obj (op (f ''·µÅ f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W')))).inv ‚â´
        (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App ((Scheme.Hom.appIso f (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W')).hom.hom)
            ((Œ≤.app (op (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W'))).hom) _ rfl (M.val.obj (op (f ''·µÅ f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W')))).hom
    case unit =>
      -- **Unit transport (?unit) ‚Äî CLOSED (iter-303).**  Goal:
      -- `restrictScalars œÅ (ùüô_ ModuleCat ùí™_X(fP)) ‚ü∂ (restr fV ùüô_X).obj W''`, with
      -- `œÅ = X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (op he.symm)) : ùí™_X(W') ‚Üí ùí™_X(fP)` the (bijective, eqToHom-
      -- induced) section-ring relabel.  This is `inv (Œµ (restrictScalars œÅ))`, supplied by the new
      -- top-level helper `unitRelabelSwap` (phrased at the `X.presheaf` CommRingCat carrier so
      -- `CommRing`/`LaxMonoidal` are native ‚Äî the direct in-place `inv Œµ` cannot be FORMED here
      -- because the `set`-local `W'` blocks call-site `CommRing ‚Üë(X.presheaf.obj (op W'))` synthesis).
      -- The `X.ringCatSheaf.map`-vs-`X.presheaf.map` and unit-section spellings reconcile by defeq.
      exact unitRelabelSwap (congrArg op he.symm)
  ¬∑ -- **naturality of the reverse component (the sole remaining hole of `sliceDualTransportInv`,
    -- iter-303 ‚Äî `app` is now fully CLOSED).**  The thin-poset square over `(Over fV)·µí·µñ`: for
    -- `f_1 : X_1 ‚ü∂ Y_1`, `restr.map f_1 ‚â´ app Y_1 = app X_1 ‚â´ (restr ùüô_X).map f_1`.  Each `app`
    -- is now the explicit 4-piece composite `M.val.map (eqToHom he) ‚â´ restrictScalars(œÅ).map
    -- (collapse ‚â´ core) ‚â´ unitRelabelSwap`; the base maps of `Opens X` agree by `Subsingleton.elim`,

    -- as named isos for `restrictScalars_collapse_apply`), then collapse the `inv Œµ`s on both sides.

    -- the slice restriction `(restr fV M.val).map f1`.  **CLOSE:** build the slice morphism

    -- monotonicity of `hle`), then `hnatœà := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply œà g'' z` (`z`'s carrier

    -- cancel as the forward's `appIso_hom_naturality_apply` close.  All infrastructure present; this
    -- thin-poset `M.val.map` alignment is the lone remaining fine-grained step.
    -- Clear the LHS collapse, peel the RHS `œà`-reindex `restrictScalars` wrapper on the pristine
    -- `.hom'` form, then clear the (now-exposed) RHS collapse.
    erw [restrictScalars_collapse_apply ((Scheme.Hom.appIso f (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (unop Y1).left)).hom.hom)
        (RingCat.Hom.hom (Œ≤.app (op (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (unop Y1).left)))) (hŒ≤ (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (unop Y1).left)) _]
    erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars.map_apply
        (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (unop X1).left)).hom)]
    erw [restrictScalars_collapse_apply ((Scheme.Hom.appIso f (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (unop X1).left)).hom.hom)
        (RingCat.Hom.hom (Œ≤.app (op (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (unop X1).left)))) (hŒ≤ (f ‚Åª¬π·µÅ (unop X1).left)) _]

      rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]

    -- are section relabels (`Y.presheaf.map jp`, `X.presheaf.map ipX` by `rfl`); the residual ring

      (Over.forget (unop V)).op.map g''

/-- **Naturality of the `.hom` direction of the structure ring iso `appIso` against restriction.**

    {U V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y} (i : (op U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ‚Ü•Y)·µí·µñ) ‚ü∂ op V) :
    X.presheaf.map ((Hom.opensFunctor f).op.map i) ‚â´ (Scheme.Hom.appIso f V).hom

  have h := Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_naturality f i

/-- **Element-level form of `appIso_hom_naturality`.**  Powers leg-B of the forward

  -- (`P = (U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W`).  Decompose it into the three legs.
  simp only [homLocalSection]
  -- The `homLocalSection`-component at `W` is the triple composite
  --   `Œ¶ = M.val.map (eqToHom e‚ÇÅ) ‚â´ (f i).val.app P ‚â´ N.val.map (eqToHom e‚ÇÇ)`  (`P = (U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W`),
  -- an `Ab`-morphism `M(W) ‚ü∂ N(W)`.  We must show `Œ¶ (r' ‚Ä¢ m') = r' ‚Ä¢ Œ¶ m'` for the structure
  -- scalar `r' = X.ringCatSheaf.map (homOfLE hWV).op r : X.ringCatSheaf(W)`.  Expose the three legs.
  erw [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
       ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
       PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_app_apply,
       PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
  -- Push the scalar through the three legs.  We use the *Œì-level* `Scheme.Modules.map_smul`
  -- (which keeps the native `Œì(M, ¬∑)`-module structure) rather than `PresheafOfModules.map_smul`
  -- (whose semilinear codomain introduces a `restrictScalars`-along-`eqToHom` module that does not
  -- match the `f`-leg's `restrictScalars ùüô` action ‚Äî `(U i).Œπ.appIso = Iso.refl`).
  -- (a) `M`-leg semilinearity (CLOSED): `M.map e‚ÇÅ (r' ‚Ä¢ m') = (X.ring.map e‚ÇÅ r') ‚Ä¢ M.map e‚ÇÅ m'`,
  -- with the native `Œì(M, image)`-action on the right (no `restrictScalars` artifact).
  erw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul M]
  -- (b) `f`-leg `(U i)`-linearity is available as the term `hfl`: `(f i).val.app P` is
  -- `(U i).ringCatSheaf(P)`-linear.  Since `(U i).Œπ.appIso = Iso.refl`
  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.Œπ_appIso`), `(U i).ringCatSheaf(P) = Œì(X, image)` and the
  -- `(U i)`-action on `M.restrict (U i).Œπ` is `ModuleCat.restrictScalars ùüô` of the native action.
  have hfl := ((f i).val.app (op ((U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ
    (Over.mk (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ‚â§ U i))).left))).hom.map_smul
  -- **Native‚Üî`restrictScalars ùüô` smul bridge** for any `K : X.Modules`.  The `(U i)`-action
  -- on `K.restrict (U i).Œπ` is `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` of the native `Œì(X, image)`-action
  -- along the structure-ring map `(forget‚ÇÇ ‚Ä¶).map ((U i).Œπ.appIso _).inv`, which is the identity
  -- because `(U i).Œπ.appIso = Iso.refl` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.Œπ_appIso`).
  have hbridge : ‚àÄ (K : X.Modules) (c : Œì(X, (U i).Œπ ''·µÅ (U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W))
      (y : Œì(K, (U i).Œπ ''·µÅ (U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W)),
      (c ‚Ä¢ y : Œì(K, (U i).Œπ ''·µÅ (U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W))
        = (c ‚Ä¢ (show ‚Üë((K.restrict (U i).Œπ).val.obj (op ((U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W))) from y)) := by
    intro K c y
    erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def']
    simp [AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.Œπ_appIso]
    rfl
  -- **Native `Œì(X, image)`-linearity of the `f`-leg**, bridged from `hfl` via `hbridge`.
  have hfl_native : ‚àÄ (c : Œì(X, (U i).Œπ ''·µÅ (U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W))
      (y : Œì(M, (U i).Œπ ''·µÅ (U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W)),
      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((f i).val.app (op ((U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W)))) (c ‚Ä¢ y)
        = c ‚Ä¢ (ConcreteCategory.hom ((f i).val.app (op ((U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W)))) y := by
    intro c y
    rw [hbridge M c y]
    erw [hfl]
    rfl
  -- (c) `N`-leg semilinearity (native), pulling the structure scalar back out.
  erw [hfl_native, Scheme.Modules.map_smul N]
  -- (d) reconcile the `eqToHom`-transported scalars: the two down-set comparison maps `e‚ÇÅ, e‚ÇÇ`
  -- compose (through the identity `(U i).Œπ.appIso`) to `ùüô` on `Œì(X, image)`, since
  -- `(U i).Œπ ''·µÅ ((U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W) = W`; on the thin poset `Opens X` this is `Subsingleton.elim`.
  congr 1
  simp only [homOfLE_leOfHom, Over.forget_obj, Over.mk_left, Functor.op_obj, sheafCompose_obj_obj,
    Functor.comp_obj, CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj, ObjectProperty.Œπ_obj, op_unop,
    Opens.Œπ_appIso, Iso.refl_inv, Functor.whiskerRight_app, CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_map_hom,
    RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe, MonoidHom.coe_coe,
    Functor.comp_map, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
  rw [‚Üê X.presheaf.map_id (op ((U i).Œπ ''·µÅ (U i).Œπ ‚Åª¬π·µÅ W))]
  erw [‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ‚Üê Functor.map_comp, ‚Üê ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
    ‚Üê Functor.map_comp]
  refine (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (congrArg X.presheaf.map
    (Subsingleton.elim _ (ùüô (op W)))) _).trans ?_
